title: Make the timeout for bindings OPTIONS request configurable
body:
## In what area(s)?

> /area runtime

## What version of Dapr?

`> 1.15.x`

see: [1.15.x](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/v1.15.0/pkg/runtime/processor/binding/send.go#L89)
see: [1.18.x](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/v1.18.1/pkg/runtime/processor/binding/send.go#L106)

## Expected Behavior

The timeout for binding `OPTIONS` discovery requests to be configurable.

## Actual Behavior

A fixed timeout of three seconds was introduced in the `1.16.x` series.  For applications that have a slower startup time (JIT), this timeout can be exceeded.  Particularly if they are running in resource constrained environments.

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

 - Configure any kind of input binding for an application that has a slow startup ( > 3 seconds )
 - When the application starts up, the Dapr `OPTIONS` probe will time out

## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Allow binding OPTIONS probe timeout to be configurable.

comments.body: Hello @atrauzzi  I will like to work on this issue, I will investigate where the OPTIONS probe timeout is configured and then plan is to make it configurable while preserving the current default behavior.
